dataset_name: PFMI Wheel Loader Synthetic IoT Dataset
description: 10-million-record synthetic sensor dataset for training Predictive Failure/Maintenance Intelligence models on heavy wheel loaders (Cat 950GC, Volvo L120H, Komatsu WA380, Hyundai HL960).

Machine_ID.pm_importance: Groups time-series per asset; required for per-machine models and fleet analytics.

Manufacturer.description: OEM brand: Caterpillar, Volvo, Komatsu, or Hyundai.
Manufacturer.pm_importance: Failure modes and operating envelopes differ by OEM; encode as embedding or one-hot.

Model.description: Specific model variant (950GC, L120H, WA380, HL960).
Model.pm_importance: Engine horsepower, bucket capacity, and component life differ by model.

Year.pm_importance: Older machines accumulate more wear; year proxies generation of components.

Engine_Hours.description: Total operating hours on the engine hour meter.
Engine_Hours.pm_importance: Primary life indicator; most OEM service intervals are hour-based.

Machine_Age.pm_importance: Ages rubber seals, wiring harnesses, and structural components independent of hours.

Serial_Number.pm_importance: Links to OEM service bulletins and recall databases.

Firmware_Version.description: ECU/telematics firmware version installed on the machine.
Firmware_Version.pm_importance: Older firmware may have known sensor bugs affecting reading accuracy.

Timestamp.description: UTC timestamp of the sensor reading.
Timestamp.pm_importance: Temporal anchor for sequence models (LSTM, Transformer); enables seasonal features.

Latitude.pm_importance: Geofencing; correlates terrain with sensor readings.

Longitude.pm_importance: Geofencing; route analysis for duty-cycle profiling.

Elevation.failure_threshold: >2,500 m — de-rating required; turbo stress increases
Elevation.pm_importance: High altitude reduces air density, stressing turbocharger and fuel system.

Ambient_Temperature.description: External air temperature at job site.
Ambient_Temperature.pm_importance: Directly affects cooling efficiency, lubricant viscosity, and battery capacity.

Humidity.pm_importance: High humidity accelerates corrosion in electrical harnesses.

Rainfall.pm_importance: Water ingress degrades hydraulic oil and electrical systems.

Dust_Level.pm_importance: Dust clogs air filters, increasing Intake_Pressure and engine wear.

Terrain_Type.description: Surface classification: Flat, Gravel, Rocky, Muddy, Sandy, Compacted, Steep, Uneven.
Terrain_Type.pm_importance: Rough/steep terrain increases suspension, axle, and tire wear rates.

Slope.pm_importance: Steep slopes dramatically increase brake temperature and transmission load.

Altitude.description: Barometric altitude (may differ slightly from GPS elevation).
Altitude.pm_importance: Used alongside GPS elevation for cross-validation and turbo stress estimation.

Weather.description: Qualitative weather condition: Clear, Cloudy, Rain, Heavy Rain, Dust Storm, Fog, Snow, Hot & Dry.
Weather.pm_importance: Encodes complex environmental interactions; useful as a context feature.

Job_Site.pm_importance: Site-specific duty cycles; some sites run machines much harder.

Engine_RPM.pm_importance: High RPM variance indicates governor issues; sustained high RPM accelerates wear.

Fuel_Rate.pm_importance: Abnormal fuel rate at given load indicates injection or combustion problems.

Fuel_Level.pm_importance: Low fuel can introduce air into fuel lines, causing cavitation damage.

Engine_Load.description: Normalized engine load factor from ECU.
Engine_Load.pm_importance: Load is the primary driver of wear rate on all mechanical components.

Hydraulic_Load.pm_importance: High hydraulic load raises pump temperature and accelerates seal degradation.

Bucket_Load.description: Estimated payload in bucket via load-moment sensor.
Bucket_Load.pm_importance: Overloading causes axle, frame fatigue, and tire failures.

Travel_Speed.failure_threshold: >35 km/h with full bucket — instability/brake stress
Travel_Speed.pm_importance: High speed with load amplifies tire wear and suspension fatigue.

Gear.pm_importance: Frequent high-gear engagement at low speed indicates operator misuse.

Idle_Time.description: Idle time within the current interval.
Idle_Time.failure_threshold: >3 min/interval consistently — excessive idling increases soot buildup
Idle_Time.pm_importance: Excessive idling causes DPF clogging and wet-stacking in diesel engines.

PTO_Status.description: Power Take-Off status: Active or Inactive.
PTO_Status.pm_importance: PTO engagement changes hydraulic and engine load profiles.

Working_Mode.description: Current operational mode: Digging, Loading, Traveling, Dumping, Idle, Grading, Stockpiling.
Working_Mode.pm_importance: Each mode has a distinct sensor signature; critical context for anomaly detection.

Operator_ID.pm_importance: Operator behavior is a major driver of wear; some operators overload consistently.

Operator_Experience.description: Operator's years of experience on heavy equipment.
Operator_Experience.pm_importance: Less experienced operators cause more thermal and mechanical stress events.

Shift.description: Current work shift: Day, Night, or Swing.
Shift.pm_importance: Night shifts have different ambient conditions; fatigue-related errors differ by shift.

Coolant_Temperature.description: Engine coolant temperature measured at radiator outlet.
Coolant_Temperature.pm_importance: Early indicator of cooling system degradation and impending engine overheating.

Oil_Temperature.pm_importance: Hot oil loses viscosity, increasing metal-to-metal contact and bearing wear.

Engine_Temperature.pm_importance: Holistic thermal health indicator; triggers ECU derate before shutdown.

Oil_Pressure.failure_threshold: <200 kPa — critical low; engine shutdown risk
Oil_Pressure.pm_importance: Pressure drops indicate pump wear, oil loss, or clogged oil pick-up strainer.

Coolant_Pressure.failure_threshold: <0.7 bar — leak suspected; >2.0 bar — cap failure
Coolant_Pressure.pm_importance: Pressure loss indicates hose/gasket leaks; cap failure causes boiling.

Intake_Air_Temperature.description: Air temperature entering the intake manifold after intercooler.
Intake_Air_Temperature.pm_importance: Hot intake air reduces combustion efficiency and is an early turbo/intercooler failure sign.

Intake_Pressure.pm_importance: Low boost at high load is the first sign of turbo compressor wheel damage.

Exhaust_Temperature.description: Exhaust gas temperature before turbocharger turbine.
Exhaust_Temperature.failure_threshold: >680°C — sustained — turbo heat damage; DPF regen issues
Exhaust_Temperature.pm_importance: EGT spikes indicate rich combustion, injector drip, or turbo restriction.

Exhaust_Backpressure.description: Pressure measured downstream of turbo turbine (DPF clogging indicator).
Exhaust_Backpressure.pm_importance: Rising backpressure reduces engine power and increases EGT.

Turbo_Boost.failure_threshold: <0.6 bar at full load — turbo failure; >2.8 bar — overboost
Turbo_Boost.pm_importance: Declining boost under load is the clearest early indicator of turbo failure.

Fuel_Pressure.failure_threshold: <300 kPa — fuel pump fault; <150 kPa — critical
Fuel_Pressure.pm_importance: Low rail pressure causes misfires, black smoke, and injector damage.

Air_Filter_Restriction.description: Air filter restriction level derived from differential pressure sensor.
Air_Filter_Restriction.pm_importance: Clogged air filter starves engine of oxygen, raising EGT and reducing power.

Hydraulic_Pressure.failure_threshold: <30 bar — pump failure suspected; >370 bar — relief valve issue
Hydraulic_Pressure.pm_importance: Pressure drops precede hydraulic pump failure; spikes indicate blockage.

Hydraulic_Temperature.description: Hydraulic oil temperature in main circuit.
Hydraulic_Temperature.pm_importance: Hot hydraulic oil degrades seals and reduces pump efficiency.

Pump_Efficiency.description: Volumetric efficiency of the main hydraulic pump (derived from flow vs. theoretical).
Pump_Efficiency.pm_importance: Gradual efficiency decline is the primary symptom of hydraulic pump wear.

Pump_Vibration.description: Vibration amplitude on hydraulic pump housing.
Pump_Vibration.failure_threshold: >8 mm/s — bearing wear inside pump; >12 — imminent failure
Pump_Vibration.pm_importance: Vibration rise precedes pump failure by days to weeks.

Pump_Current.description: Electric current drawn by hydraulic pump motor (if electric-driven).
Pump_Current.failure_threshold: >75 A — overload; <5 A at work — seized pump
Pump_Current.pm_importance: Current spikes indicate stalling; drops indicate loss of hydraulic prime.

Hydraulic_Flow.description: Flow rate in main hydraulic circuit.
Hydraulic_Flow.failure_threshold: <20 L/min at full load — pump fault
Hydraulic_Flow.pm_importance: Flow drop at constant pressure indicates internal pump leakage.

Hydraulic_Oil_Level.pm_importance: Low level causes pump cavitation and rapid seal failure.

Battery_Voltage.pm_importance: Voltage sag indicates aging batteries or charging system faults.

Battery_Current.failure_threshold: <-20 A at idle — excessive parasitic drain
Battery_Current.pm_importance: Excessive discharge without regen indicates short circuits or failed components.

Alternator_Output.failure_threshold: <24 V — alternator undercharging; >31 V — regulator fault
Alternator_Output.pm_importance: Undercharging leads to battery depletion and ECU resets in the field.

ECU_Temperature.pm_importance: Hot ECU causes intermittent faults and sensor reading errors.

Transmission_Temperature.pm_importance: Hot transmission fluid leads to clutch pack slippage and gear failure.

Transmission_Pressure.pm_importance: Pressure drop correlates directly with clutch pack wear progression.

Brake_Temperature.description: Wet disc brake oil temperature (or caliper temp on dry brakes).
Brake_Temperature.pm_importance: Overheated brakes lose stopping power rapidly — critical safety failure.

Brake_Pressure.failure_threshold: <40 bar — fluid loss or actuator failure
Brake_Pressure.pm_importance: Pressure decay without braking indicates internal cylinder seal failure.

Tire_Pressure_FL.pm_importance: Underinflated tires generate heat, deform sidewalls, and fail catastrophically.

Tire_Pressure_FR.pm_importance: Pressure imbalance between sides indicates puncture or bead damage.

Tire_Pressure_RL.pm_importance: Rear tires carry drive torque; low pressure causes bead slip on rim.

Tire_Pressure_RR.pm_importance: Monitor all four together; rapid loss in any one indicates puncture.

Tire_Temperature.description: Average tire sidewall surface temperature (IR sensor).
Tire_Temperature.failure_threshold: >90°C — heat buildup from underinflation or overloading
Tire_Temperature.pm_importance: High tire temps with low pressure signal imminent blowout.

Steering_Angle.description: Articulation angle of the front/rear frame (±55° max).
Steering_Angle.pm_importance: Frequent high-angle steering at speed stresses the articulation bearing.

Suspension_Vibration.description: Whole-machine vibration measured at frame near operator cab.
Suspension_Vibration.failure_threshold: >9 g — structural fatigue concern; >12 g — immediate check
Suspension_Vibration.pm_importance: Sustained high-g vibration cracks welds and fatigues hydraulic hoses.

Overall_Vibration.description: Broadband vibration velocity at drivetrain measurement point.
Overall_Vibration.pm_importance: Broadband vibration increase is the first symptom of any mechanical degradation.

FFT_Peak.description: Dominant frequency component peak from FFT vibration analysis.
FFT_Peak.pm_importance: Frequency content identifies specific fault types (BPFO, BPFI, FTF for bearings).

RMS_Vibration.pm_importance: RMS is the ISO-standard severity measure; trend analysis detects bearing wear early.

Bearing_Temperature.description: Temperature of main drive or wheel bearing housing.
Bearing_Temperature.pm_importance: Friction from worn bearings generates heat; temperature rise lags vibration.

Bearing_Vibration.description: Vibration signal filtered to bearing fault frequency bands.
Bearing_Vibration.pm_importance: Most sensitive early-warning signal for bearing degradation.

Acoustic_Noise_Level.description: Overall acoustic emission measured at 1 meter from machine.
Acoustic_Noise_Level.pm_importance: Knocking or grinding sounds appear before structural failures.

Ultrasonic_Emission.description: Ultrasonic acoustic emission level (40–100 kHz band) for early bearing/gear defect detection.
Ultrasonic_Emission.pm_importance: Ultrasonic emission detects micro-cracks and early spalling weeks before vibration rises.

Last_Service_Days.description: Days since last scheduled preventive maintenance.
Last_Service_Days.pm_importance: Overdue service dramatically increases failure probability across all systems.

Last_Service_Hours.description: Engine hours accumulated since last service.
Last_Service_Hours.failure_threshold: >500 h — overdue by typical PM interval
Last_Service_Hours.pm_importance: Hour-based trigger for oil, filter, and lubrication service needs.

Lubrication_Status.description: Qualitative assessment of lubrication system: Good, Fair, Poor.
Lubrication_Status.pm_importance: Poor lubrication rapidly accelerates bearing and gear surface fatigue.

Filter_Age.description: Hours since last air/fuel/hydraulic filter replacement.
Filter_Age.pm_importance: Old filters increase system contamination and restriction.

Oil_Age.description: Hours since last engine oil change.
Oil_Age.failure_threshold: >500 h — oil degradation; TBN likely depleted
Oil_Age.pm_importance: Degraded oil increases engine wear rate by 3–5× compared to fresh oil.

Previous_Failure_Count.description: Total count of prior failures recorded for this machine.
Previous_Failure_Count.failure_threshold: >5 — recurrent failure pattern; systematic inspection needed
Previous_Failure_Count.pm_importance: Prior failures indicate structural or design-life issues that predict future events.

Parts_Replaced.description: Most recently replaced component category: None, Filters, Oil, Belts, Bearings, Pump, Tires.
Parts_Replaced.pm_importance: Replacement resets component degradation; needed to avoid false positives post-maintenance.

Warranty_Status.description: Machine warranty status: Active or Expired.
Warranty_Status.pm_importance: Expired warranty machines may have deferred maintenance; correlates with failure rate.

Active_DTC.description: Pipe-separated list of active Diagnostic Trouble Codes (J1939 SAE format).
Active_DTC.pm_importance: DTCs directly encode ECU-detected faults; strong label for multi-label classification.

Warning_Level.description: Synthesized warning severity: None, Low, Medium, High, Critical.
Warning_Level.pm_importance: Pre-computed severity label useful as auxiliary training signal.

Error_Code.description: Primary DTC code string (most severe active code).
Error_Code.pm_importance: Enables rule-based baseline comparison with ML predictions.

Fault_Category.description: System category of active fault: Mechanical, Hydraulic, Electrical, Thermal, None.
Fault_Category.pm_importance: Subsystem routing label; useful for hierarchical classification.

Failure_Within_24h.description: Label: 1 if a failure will occur within the next 24 hours.
Failure_Within_24h.pm_importance: PRIMARY LABEL for urgent failure prediction (binary classifier).

Failure_Within_7d.description: Label: 1 if a failure will occur within the next 7 days.
Failure_Within_7d.pm_importance: PRIMARY LABEL for week-ahead maintenance scheduling.

Remaining_Useful_Life.description: Estimated hours until the next failure event. Null when no failure arc is active.
Remaining_Useful_Life.pm_importance: REGRESSION TARGET for continuous RUL prediction (survival analysis, LSTM-RUL).

Maintenance_Action.description: Recommended maintenance urgency: Routine, Soon, Urgent.
Maintenance_Action.pm_importance: Multi-class classification target for maintenance prioritization systems.

Failure_Type.description: Type of impending or active failure: one of 11 failure classes or 'No Failure'.
Failure_Type.pm_importance: MULTI-CLASS LABEL for fault type identification — enables targeted repair dispatch.
